methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 9 same-sector + same/adjacent-stage peers in Robotics & Manufacturing. Range: $300M - $10.0B.

private.name: Hadrian
private.rationale: Same sector (Robotics & Manufacturing), same stage (Series C), post-money $1.6B

private.name: Nimble Robotics
private.rationale: Same sector (Robotics & Manufacturing), same stage (Series C), post-money $1B

data.narrative:
Agile Robots is a Series C robotics and manufacturing company seeking valuation assessment. The peer universe comprises nine same-sector, same/adjacent-stage comparables: one publicly traded (277810.KQ) and eight private firms including 1X Technologies ($10.0B Series B), Exotec ($2.0B Series D), Bedrock Robotics ($1.8B Series B), Dexterity ($1.6B Series B), and Hadrian ($1.6B Series C).

The peer-median method is the applicable triangulation approach given data availability. The median valuation across the nine-company peer set is $1.6B, with a range of $300M to $10.0B. This method directly addresses same-sector positioning and stage comparability, though the wide dispersion reflects heterogeneity in business models and capital efficiency within the robotics sector.

The triangulated valuation analysis supports a range of $1.4B (low) to $1.8B (high), centered on $1.6B (median). This aligns closely with the target's stated pre-money valuation above $1B and reflects capital raised of approximately $384M to date. Key uncertainties include revenue generation profile, customer concentration, and technical differentiation relative to the peer set—factors not quantified in available data but material to Stage C risk pricing.
